@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KS5F7BR6850RKT67X4CNHPAZ
+- 01KS5F5ZNA0621X8KM2NPERXNV
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: 9b80
+project: command-service
+title: 'Change propagation: undo/redo emit the same events as edits (caches + UI notified generically)'
+---
+## What
+
+Make undo/redo (and any store write) propagate to every dependent тАФ in-process caches and the UI тАФ through **one** change-event path, so nothing downstream special-cases undo. The principle: **an undo is indistinguishable from a forward edit downstream.** A dependent must not need to know whether a change came from a user edit, an agent, or an undo тАФ it reacts to the same event.
+
+This generalizes today's `reconcile_post_undo_caches` (in `app_commands.rs`, which manually refreshes the entity + perspective + view caches after undo) into an event-driven flow over the shared `StoreContext`.
+
+### The flow
+
+1. **Emit on undo/redo.** `StoreContext::undo`/`redo` already returns `UndoOutcome { items: [(store, item)тАж] }` and rewrites bytes via `reverse_patch`/`forward_patch`. Extend it to **emit a thin `ChangeEvent` per touched item** through the same channel `flush_all()` aggregates тАФ the same events a create/update/delete emits. Use the entity changelog's field-diff (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/changelog.rs`) so field-level events carry `{field, value}`, matching the existing thin-event model (entity-level create/remove + field-level field/value; no enrichment reads, no re-fetch round-trips).
+
+2. **In-process caches subscribe.** The `kanban` entity cache and the `views`/perspective cache subscribe to the shared `StoreContext` change stream and reload exactly the items named in each event. No server calls another server's MCP; they share the `Arc<StoreContext>` and consume its event stream. Replaces the bespoke post-undo reconciliation with a generic subscription that also covers normal edits, watcher-driven external edits, and agent writes.
+
+3. **UI notified via MCP notifications.** A notification bridge forwards the substrate change events as MCP `notifications/тАжchanged` to subscribed clients. The kanban frontend already subscribes (via the `mcp_subscribe` transport) to these for normal edits, so an undo re-renders the UI with **zero undo-specific frontend code**.
+
+Files:
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/src/context.rs` тАФ `undo`/`redo` emit change events for `UndoOutcome.items` (extend, don't rewrite)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` тАФ subscribe their caches to the shared change stream; drop the manual post-undo reconcile once the subscription covers it
+- the MCP notification bridge (wherever `mcp_subscribe` is served тАФ `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs` + the server layer) тАФ forward substrate change events as notifications
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/app_commands.rs` тАФ delete `reconcile_post_undo_caches` once the event-driven path replaces it (its logic moves to the subscription)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `store.undo`/`store.redo` emit thin change events for every item in `UndoOutcome.items`, identical in shape to edit-driven events
+- [ ] The `kanban` entity cache and `views`/perspective cache refresh from those events with no undo-specific code path
+- [ ] The UI is notified of undo-driven changes through the same MCP notification it already consumes for edits тАФ no new frontend subscription needed
+- [ ] `reconcile_post_undo_caches` is removed; its responsibility lives in the generic subscription
+- [ ] A dependent cannot tell an undo from an edit (same event shape) тАФ asserted by test
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/tests/integration/undo_emits_events_e2e.rs` тАФ undo a task edit; assert a change event is emitted matching the shape of the original edit's event
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/undo_notifies_dependents_e2e.rs` тАФ boot host with `kanban` + `views` + `store`; edit an entity and a perspective; `store.undo` twice; assert (a) the in-process caches reflect the reverted state without manual refresh, (b) a subscribed MCP client received `notifications/тАжchanged` for the reverted items
+- [ ] Frontend: `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-notification.test.tsx` тАФ mock MCP transport; emit the change notification that an undo produces; assert the board re-renders the reverted state (same handler as a normal edit)
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-store && cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration undo_notifies_dependents_e2e` pass
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` тАФ write `undo_notifies_dependents_e2e.rs` first; it pins the "undo == edit, downstream" contract.
+
+Depends on the `store` server task and the shared-substrate task. Closely related to the event-architecture rule (thin events). Coordinates with the `single-changelog` project (unifying the per-store changelog formats makes the emitted events uniform).
\ No newline at end of file

 ---
 ## What

-Make undo/redo (and any store write) propagate to every dependent тАФ in-process caches and the UI тАФ through **one** change-event path, so nothing downstream special-cases undo. The principle: **an undo is indistinguishable from a forward edit downstream.** A dependent must not need to know whether a change came from a user edit, an agent, or an undo тАФ it reacts to the same event.
+Make undo AND redo propagate to every dependent тАФ in-process caches and the UI тАФ through the paths they already use, so nothing downstream special-cases undo/redo. There are **two** distinct notifications, and redo makes the distinction obvious:
+
+### A. Data changes (symmetric undo/redo) тАФ derive the event from the byte transition
+
+`StoreContext::undo` applies `reverse_patch`; `redo` applies `forward_patch`. Both return `UndoOutcome { items: [(store, item)тАж] }`. After the rewrite, emit a **thin change event per item describing the actual transition on disk**, NOT a blanket "update":
+- none тЖТ content = `entity created`
+- content тЖТ none = `entity removed`
+- content тЖТ content = field-level `{field, value}` changes (diff via `swissarmyhammer-entity/src/changelog.rs`)

-This generalizes today's `reconcile_post_undo_caches` (in `app_commands.rs`, which manually refreshes the entity + perspective + view caches after undo) into an event-driven flow over the shared `StoreContext`.
+This single direction-agnostic derivation serves both undo and redo. It is why redo-of-a-create emits `created` and redo-of-a-delete emits `removed` тАФ if we blindly emitted "update", a redo-of-delete would leave a ghost card in the UI. Events match the existing thin-event model (entity create/remove + field/value; no re-fetch).

-### The flow
+Consumers (unchanged by direction):
+- **In-process caches** (`kanban` entity cache, `views`/perspective cache) subscribe to the shared `StoreContext` change stream and reload the named items. Replaces the bespoke `reconcile_post_undo_caches` and also covers normal edits, agent writes, and watcher-driven external edits.
+- **UI** via the MCP notification bridge тЖТ `notifications/тАжchanged`; the frontend already subscribes (`mcp_subscribe`) for normal edits, so undo/redo re-render with zero direction-specific code.

-1. **Emit on undo/redo.** `StoreContext::undo`/`redo` already returns `UndoOutcome { items: [(store, item)тАж] }` and rewrites bytes via `reverse_patch`/`forward_patch`. Extend it to **emit a thin `ChangeEvent` per touched item** through the same channel `flush_all()` aggregates тАФ the same events a create/update/delete emits. Use the entity changelog's field-diff (`swissarmyhammer-entity/src/changelog.rs`) so field-level events carry `{field, value}`, matching the existing thin-event model (entity-level create/remove + field-level field/value; no enrichment reads, no re-fetch round-trips).
+### B. Undo-stack state event (its own concern тАФ fires on push/undo/redo)

-2. **In-process caches subscribe.** The `kanban` entity cache and the `views`/perspective cache subscribe to the shared `StoreContext` change stream and reload exactly the items named in each event. No server calls another server's MCP; they share the `Arc<StoreContext>` and consume its event stream. Replaces the bespoke post-undo reconciliation with a generic subscription that also covers normal edits, watcher-driven external edits, and agent writes.
+Undo/redo also change `can_undo`/`can_redo` and the next undo/redo labels тАФ state the UI's Undo/Redo controls depend on, separate from entity data. The `store` server emits a **stack-state notification on every stack mutation** тАФ `push`, `undo`, `redo` тАФ carrying `{ can_undo, can_redo, undo_label?, redo_label? }` (labels peeked from the `UndoStack` entry at/around the pointer).

-3. **UI notified via MCP notifications.** A notification bridge forwards the substrate change events as MCP `notifications/тАжchanged` to subscribed clients. The kanban frontend already subscribes (via the `mcp_subscribe` transport) to these for normal edits, so an undo re-renders the UI with **zero undo-specific frontend code**.
+Critical non-symmetric case: a **normal edit after an undo discards the redo tail** (`UndoStack::push` truncates at the pointer), so `can_redo` flips to false with no undo/redo call тАФ just an edit. The stack-state event must therefore fire on plain writes too, or the Redo control stays wrongly enabled. Data events never carry this; it is a property of the stack, not any item.

 Files:
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/src/context.rs` тАФ `undo`/`redo` emit change events for `UndoOutcome.items` (extend, don't rewrite)
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` тАФ subscribe their caches to the shared change stream; drop the manual post-undo reconcile once the subscription covers it
-- the MCP notification bridge (wherever `mcp_subscribe` is served тАФ `apps/kanban-app/src/commands/mcp.rs` + the server layer) тАФ forward substrate change events as notifications
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/app_commands.rs` тАФ delete `reconcile_post_undo_caches` once the event-driven path replaces it (its logic moves to the subscription)
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/src/context.rs` тАФ `undo`/`redo` emit transition-derived data events for `UndoOutcome.items`; `push`/`undo`/`redo` emit the stack-state event
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-entity/src/context.rs`, `crates/swissarmyhammer-views/src/context.rs` тАФ subscribe caches to the shared change stream; drop manual post-undo reconcile
+- MCP notification bridge (where `mcp_subscribe` is served) тАФ forward both the data events and the stack-state event as notifications
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/commands/app_commands.rs` тАФ remove `reconcile_post_undo_caches` (replaced by the subscription)

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `store.undo`/`store.redo` emit thin change events for every item in `UndoOutcome.items`, identical in shape to edit-driven events
-- [ ] The `kanban` entity cache and `views`/perspective cache refresh from those events with no undo-specific code path
-- [ ] The UI is notified of undo-driven changes through the same MCP notification it already consumes for edits тАФ no new frontend subscription needed
-- [ ] `reconcile_post_undo_caches` is removed; its responsibility lives in the generic subscription
-- [ ] A dependent cannot tell an undo from an edit (same event shape) тАФ asserted by test
+- [ ] undo and redo emit data events derived from the actual byte transition (create/remove/field), not a blanket update
+- [ ] redo-of-a-delete emits `removed`; redo-of-a-create emits `created`; redo-of-an-update emits the new field values
+- [ ] in-process caches refresh from those events with no direction-specific code; `reconcile_post_undo_caches` is gone
+- [ ] a stack-state notification fires on push, undo, AND redo with correct `can_undo`/`can_redo`/labels
+- [ ] a normal edit after an undo fires a stack-state event with `can_redo:false` (redo tail discarded)
+- [ ] the UI cannot tell undo from redo from edit for data; its Undo/Redo controls track the stack-state event

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/tests/integration/undo_emits_events_e2e.rs` тАФ undo a task edit; assert a change event is emitted matching the shape of the original edit's event
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/undo_notifies_dependents_e2e.rs` тАФ boot host with `kanban` + `views` + `store`; edit an entity and a perspective; `store.undo` twice; assert (a) the in-process caches reflect the reverted state without manual refresh, (b) a subscribed MCP client received `notifications/тАжchanged` for the reverted items
-- [ ] Frontend: `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-notification.test.tsx` тАФ mock MCP transport; emit the change notification that an undo produces; assert the board re-renders the reverted state (same handler as a normal edit)
-- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-store && cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration undo_notifies_dependents_e2e` pass
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/tests/integration/undo_redo_emits_events_e2e.rs` тАФ createтЖТundo emits `removed`; redo emits `created`; updateтЖТundo emits old field values, redo emits new; assert event op-types match the transition
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-store/tests/integration/stack_state_events_e2e.rs` тАФ push fires stack event (can_undo true); undo fires (can_redo true); redo fires (can_redo false); a fresh edit after an undo fires with can_redo false
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-command-service/tests/integration/undo_redo_notifies_dependents_e2e.rs` тАФ boot `kanban`+`views`+`store`; edit entity + perspective; undo then redo; assert caches reflect each state without manual refresh AND a subscribed MCP client received both the data events and the stack-state events
+- [ ] Frontend: `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/.../undo-redo-notification.test.tsx` тАФ emit the data + stack events a redo produces; assert the board re-renders the reapplied state and the Undo/Redo buttons enable/disable correctly
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-store && cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-command-service --test integration undo_redo_notifies_dependents_e2e` pass

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` тАФ write `undo_notifies_dependents_e2e.rs` first; it pins the "undo == edit, downstream" contract.
+- Use `/tdd` тАФ write `undo_redo_emits_events_e2e.rs` (transition-derivation) and `stack_state_events_e2e.rs` first; they pin the two contracts redo exposes.

-Depends on the `store` server task and the shared-substrate task. Closely related to the event-architecture rule (thin events). Coordinates with the `single-changelog` project (unifying the per-store changelog formats makes the emitted events uniform).
\ No newline at end of file
+Depends on the `store` server and shared-substrate tasks. Related to the event-architecture rule (thin events) and the `single-changelog` project.
